Ayurvedic Remedies 2:
Low-Calorie Diet:

Choose whole grains instead of refined foods

Ayurvedic Remedies1:
Natural / Herbal Support (Not a cure):
No herbal or natural remedy can cure bladder cancer
Supportive dietary options:
Fruits and vegetables (antioxidants)
Adequate hydration (helps urinary system health)
Green tea (general health support)
Ayurvedic Remedies 2:
Medical Treatment:
Surgery (removal of tumor or bladder in severe cases)
Immunotherapy (e.g., BCG therapy)
Chemotherapy (intravesical or systemic)
Radiation therapy (in some cases)
Targeted therapy (for advanced stages)
Lifestyle Support:
Stop smoking (major risk factor reduction)
Drink plenty of water
Maintain healthy diet and weight
Regular medical follow-ups and cystoscopy checks
overall: Overall: Bladder cancer requires specialist medical treatment such as surgery and cancer therapy. Natural remedies cannot treat or cure it, but healthy lifestyle habits may support recovery and overall well-being during treatment.
Diet and Lifestyle Recommendations: Drink plenty of water, avoid bladder irritants like caffeine and alcohol.

Ayurvedic Remedies1:
Natural / Herbal Remedies:
No herbal or natural cure exists for cleft lip and palate
Supportive care (especially in infancy):
Proper nutrition (breastfeeding support or special feeding bottles)
Good oral hygiene to prevent infections
Ayurvedic Remedies 2:

Surgery to repair cleft lip (usually in early infancy)

Speech therapy to improve speaking ability

ENT care for ear/hearing issues if needed
Lifestyle Support:
Regular follow-ups with pediatric specialist
Emotional and psychological support for child and family
Proper nutrition for growth and healing
overall: Overall: Cleft lip and palate is a congenital condition that requires surgical correction and medical care. Natural remedies cannot treat it, but supportive care helps improve health and development before and after surgery.
Diet and Lifestyle Recommendations: Follow speech therapy, eat soft foods, and avoid harsh chemicals.

Symptoms: Blisters on lips or around the mouth
Ayurvedic Remedies1:
Aloe Vera:
Soothes irritation and burning sensation
Helps reduce redness and inflammation
Supports faster skin healing
Use: Apply aloe vera gel directly on the affected area 2–3 times daily
Ayurvedic Remedies 2:
Lemon Balm:
Has antiviral properties (may help herpes simplex virus symptoms)
Can reduce itching, swelling, and discomfort
Supports healing of cold sores
Use: Apply lemon balm cream or cooled tea extract on the affected area
overall: Overall: Aloe vera and lemon balm are natural remedies that may help relieve pain, itching, and inflammation caused by cold sores. They are supportive treatments and do not cure the virus, but may help speed up healing and reduce symptoms.
Diet and Lifestyle Recommendations: Avoid stress, apply a cold compress to the affected area, eat antiviral foods.

Symptoms: Ear pain, fluid drainage, hearing loss
Diagnosis & Tests: Physical Exam, Ear Swab, Hearing Test
Ayurvedic Remedies1:
Garlic:
Has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties
May help reduce infection-related discomfort (supportive use only)
Use: Best taken in diet (eating garlic). Do not put raw garlic directly into the ear as it may irritate or damage the ear canal
Ayurvedic Remedies 2:
Warm Compress:
Helps reduce ear pain and discomfort
Improves blood circulation and relieves pressure
Use: Apply a warm cloth gently on the affected ear for 10–15 minutes
overall: Overall: Garlic and warm compress may help ease symptoms of mild ear discomfort, but ear infections often require medical treatment (especially antibiotics or ear drops prescribed by a doctor). Avoid inserting anything into the ear without medical advice.
Diet and Lifestyle Recommendations: Avoid water in the ears, maintain good ear hygiene, and avoid cold air.

Use: Drink fresh lemon water regularly (without excess sugar)

May help dissolve small kidney stones in some cases

overall: Overall: Lemon juice and apple cider vinegar may help support kidney health and reduce the risk of stone formation, but kidney stones often require medical evaluation and treatment depending on size and severity. Drinking plenty of water is the most important preventive step.

Symptoms: High fever, rash, joint pain, muscle pain
Risk Factors: Mosquito Bites, Travel to Endemic Areas
Ayurvedic Remedies1:
Natural / Herbal Support (Not a cure):
No herbal or natural remedy can cure dengue fever
Supportive options:
Papaya leaf extract (commonly used to support platelet levels, evidence is limited)
Coconut water (helps maintain hydration and electrolytes)
Giloy / Tulsi (traditionally used for immune support)
Ayurvedic Remedies 2:
Medical Care (Very Important):
No specific antiviral cure; treatment is supportive
Fever and pain management under doctor guidance (paracetamol is commonly used)
IV fluids if dehydration is severe
Platelet and blood monitoring
Hospitalization in severe cases (Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever)
Lifestyle & Support:
Strict rest
Drink plenty of fluids (water, ORS, soups)
Avoid aspirin/ibuprofen unless prescribed (can increase bleeding risk)
Regular monitoring of warning signs (bleeding, severe weakness)
overall: Overall: Dengue fever requires careful medical monitoring and supportive hospital treatment in severe cases. Natural remedies may help support hydration and general recovery, but they cannot cure dengue. Early diagnosis and proper medical care are very important.
Diet and Lifestyle Recommendations: Rest, stay hydrated, and avoid mosquito bites.
